title: NOC 21230 – Computer Systems Developers and Programmers | GTR Immigration
description: Computer Systems Developers and Programmers are in strong demand across Canada, driven by rapid growth in software engineering, scientific computing, digital transformation, fintech platforms, and public-sector IT modernization. Classified under NOC 21230 (TEER 1), this occupation qualifies for Express-Entry-linked programs, Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs), LMIA-supported work permits, and long-term Permanent Residence (PR) pathways.

classification.category: 2 – Natural and applied sciences and related occupations
classification.teer: 1 – Occupations usually require a university degree
classification.major_group: 212 – Professional occupations in applied sciences (except engineering)
classification.minor_group: 2123 – Computer, software, and web designers and developers

duties: Computer Systems Developers and Programmers typically:
Write, modify, integrate, and test software code
Diagnose technical problems and propose solutions
employment_requirements: Bachelor’s degree or college diploma in computer science, engineering, or related field
Specialized study or experience for scientific or engineering systems programming
job_outlook: This keeps the occupation immigration-friendly, especially for overseas applicants with:
additional_information: Canada’s expanding technology sector continues to fuel recruitment for:
Systems-level software developmentScientific & engineering applicationsOperating-system programmingEmbedded platformsFinancial-technology solutions
Typical annual salaries range from $45,406–$113,458 CAD, depending on experience, province, and technical specialization.
